text: For flights to or from the UK, passengers must comply with Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs (DEFRA) requirements: notify the airline at least 48 hours before departure, provide training certification from an accredited organization (ADI, IGDF, or ADAA) or proof of equivalent standards, and provide a written declaration detailing the specific tasks the dog performs. Carrying an Assistance Dog ID card is recommended to facilitate airport verification.
For flights to or from Ireland, assistance dogs arriving from EU or Schengen countries require proof of tapeworm treatment.

For flights to or from Ireland, assistance dogs arriving from countries outside the EU or Schengen area require tapeworm treatment, a blood test confirming the treatment's effectiveness, and an email notification sent to petmove@agriculture.gov.ie.

Passengers arriving in Ireland from countries outside the EU or Schengen area must notify the Ministry of Agriculture, Food and the Marine via the Pet Import Portal at least 24 hours before the scheduled arrival time.

Assistance dogs must be clean, well-behaved, and under the passenger's control at all times. During takeoff and landing, the dog must remain on the floor under the seat and be secured with a safety harness. Dogs must not block aisles or emergency evacuation areas. Passengers must provide all necessary care items, such as water bowls, blankets, absorbent pads, and sanitary bags.
Vueling provides the Hearlo app, a free tool designed to facilitate communication between airline staff and passengers with hearing impairments, speech disorders, or language barriers.
Vueling provides comprehensive information and support for passengers with any type of disability or reduced mobility to facilitate their travel.

Airport assistance is available for passengers who use wheelchairs, those with limbs in plaster casts, individuals with mobility, sight, or hearing impairments, and passengers with intellectual disabilities.

Passengers who are not self-sufficient must always travel with a companion. Similarly, airport assistance for minors is only provided if they are accompanied. Additional information is available for passengers traveling with children with autism.

To ensure service quality and availability, assistance should be requested at least 48 hours before flight departure. Failure to provide advance notice may result in the unavailability of required assistance, potentially impacting travel plans.

Passengers can request assistance during the booking process on the Vueling website by specifying their needs and whether they are traveling with an assistance dog. If the booking was made via the app or the need was not specified during purchase, requests can be submitted through the 'REQUEST ASSISTANCE' section of the website.

Dedicated phone lines for passengers with special needs are available: Spain (+34 931222230), France (+33 185148077), Italy (+39 0694802424), UK (+44 2045710521), and the rest of the world (+34 931222251).

Vueling provides informative articles for passengers with various special needs, including larger passengers requiring an extra seat, pregnant passengers, children and infants, unaccompanied minors, traveling with pets, and traveling with medication.

Assistance or service dogs are individually trained to help people with special needs. Emotional support animals are not globally recognized as service animals. To travel with an assistance dog, passengers must indicate this during the booking process in the Passenger details section by selecting the 'Special needs passengers' option.

Passengers should notify Vueling at least 48 hours before flight departure via the Customer Service Centre, virtual assistant, or the exclusive assistance service lines: Spain (+34 931222230), France (+33 185148077), Italy (+39 0694802424), UK (+44 2045710521), or the rest of the world (+34 931222251). Notifications made less than 48 hours in advance will be handled on a best-effort basis.

In accordance with internal safety procedures, the maximum number of assistance dogs allowed on board is two. This limit may only be exceeded in exceptional cases, making early requests essential for proper management.

To validate required documentation before a flight, passengers should contact the Customer Service Centre via the virtual assistant or the exclusive assistance service. An official physical certificate qualifying the animal as an assistance or guide dog is required; a doctor's note is not sufficient to certify the need for an assistance animal.

Assistance dogs must be certified by an accredited organization, such as Assistance Dogs International (ADI), International Guide Dog Federation (IGDF), Assistance Dogs Assessment Association (ADAA), or the Spanish National Organization for the Blind (ONCE). Dogs without an official certificate will not be admitted on board.

Assistance dogs must be at least 3 months old and identified by a tattoo or microchip. Passengers must carry the dog's immunization record, certifying a rabies vaccination administered at least 21 days before the flight.

For travel to Sweden, Ireland, and the United Kingdom, a tapeworm treatment is required. The veterinarian must provide the product name, manufacturer, date and time of treatment, and a stamp and signature.
For international flights, an animal passport is required. Passengers traveling outside the European Union should verify specific travel requirements with the consulate of the destination country.
Hearlo is designed to improve communication and create a more accessible, autonomous airport experience for passengers, reducing the need for interpreters.

Hearlo provides several tools to facilitate communication: 
- Messaging: Generates audio from written text, supporting up to 40 languages.
- Hearlo Cards: Generates audio from pictograms related to airport activities such as check-in and boarding.
- Shortcuts: Generates audio from predefined phrases linked to airport activities.
- Phrases: Allows the construction of complex sentences using a combination of text and pictograms to generate audio.
- Tours: Provides step-by-step audio descriptions of the travel process, including airport arrival, check-in, security, boarding, the flight, and disembarking.
The Hearlo app can be used at the airport and on board the aircraft. It functions without an internet connection, provided the app is downloaded before the trip.
To obtain the Hearlo app, passengers can scan the provided QR code or use the direct download link. After installation, users can select the communication method that best suits their needs.

Passengers may book an Extra Seat for additional space and privacy, or when traveling with a musical instrument or a plaster cast. The extra seat is always adjacent to the passenger's allocated seat and can be booked for any area of the aircraft, excluding emergency exits.

section: Extra Seat for Physical and Medical Needs
text: Larger passengers may request double space by booking an extra seat. Passengers with a plaster cast who are unable to bend their knee must book two adjoining seats through Customer Services in addition to their own seat.
Stretchers are not permitted on board Vueling flights.
Passengers requiring assistance can go directly to the Vueling check-in desks, and the airline will notify the airport assistance service of their arrival.

To ensure maximum comfort, customers with reduced mobility are the first to board the aircraft. Depending on the specific assistance requested, the airport service will escort the passenger to the boarding gate, the aircraft door, or directly to their seat.

Vueling provides onboard wheelchairs across its entire fleet to assist passengers with reduced mobility when moving within the cabin. Additionally, safety leaflets in braille are available on all aircraft upon request.
Upon landing, assisted customers will be the last to disembark the aircraft and will be escorted by ground staff to their designated location.
Vueling provides specific guidelines and information for passengers traveling with an assistance dog.
If mobility equipment is delayed, damaged, or lost, the incident must be reported to the Lost and Found office before the passenger leaves the arrivals terminal.
Passengers traveling with a wheelchair or mobility aid may check in up to two mobility devices free of charge (for example, a wheelchair and a walker).

Passengers with battery-powered mobility aids must verify transport restrictions based on the battery type. It is recommended to consult the manufacturer's instructions for details regarding battery type, dimensions, weight, and the procedure for disconnecting the battery.

For safety reasons, battery-powered wheelchairs or mobility aids must be verified at the airport before being accepted on board. Passengers should provide documentation covering the device type, brand, model, weight, dimensions, battery type (including watt-hours for lithium batteries), and instructions on how to isolate the battery to prevent accidental activation. Transport may be refused if the device does not meet these requirements.

Accepted battery types include non-spillable dry batteries (including integrated), gel batteries, and wet (sealed) batteries. Battery terminals and electrical circuits must be isolated to prevent accidental activation, and batteries must be securely attached or removed according to manufacturer instructions. Passengers are permitted a maximum of one spare battery with no power limitation.

Passengers must be self-sufficient in the following areas: understanding safety instructions, fastening and unfastening seat belts, locating and putting on life jackets, exiting their seats to reach emergency exits, using oxygen masks, and using the toilet independently (as accessible toilets are not available).

Passengers unable to meet the self-sufficiency safety requirements must travel with a non-disabled adult companion (18 years or older) who can provide the necessary assistance. Failure to do so will result in denied boarding.

section: Booking and Seat Assignment for Special Needs
text: Passengers with special needs and one companion are entitled to a free adapted seat. These can be assigned during online check-in (starting 7 days before departure), by requesting from the crew on the day of the flight, or by calling the following dedicated numbers: Spain (+931222230), France (+33185148077), Italy (+390694802424), UK (+442045710521), or the Rest of the World (+34931222251).
To ensure suitable seat placement and facilitate traveling together, passengers are recommended to check in as early as possible within the available time frame.

section: Aircraft Capacity Limits for Special Assistance
text: Internal security procedures limit the total number of passengers with special needs (accompanied or unaccompanied) and their companions to 10% of the aircraft's capacity. This limit may be exceeded for groups on an exceptional basis if requested in advance via the Customer Service Centre.

Passengers with disabilities or reduced mobility are typically allocated window seats; requests for reassignment should be directed to the Customer Services Centre. Seats in the first or last rows, and those at or near emergency exits, cannot be occupied by these passengers.

section: Additional Seat Requirements for Plaster Casts
text: Specific seat booking requirements apply for passengers with plaster casts: those with casts from the waist up require one seat, while those with casts from the waist down (whole leg) must book three seats.

section: Additional Seat Requirements for Plaster Casts
text: Passengers with a plaster cast only on the lower leg must book two seats to travel.

Passengers using airport assistance are allowed one free onboard bag (max 10 kg, 55x40x20 cm) and one underseat cabin bag (max 40x20x30 cm), with the option to check these in for free. Permitted onboard items include crutches, walking sticks, umbrellas, walking frames, and apnea machines. Medicine or diabetes kits are allowed in cabin bags provided a medical certificate is presented.

Passengers who have requested assistance should arrive at the airport meeting point at least 2.5 hours before the scheduled departure. Because departure and arrival services are provided by the airport and vary by destination, passengers are encouraged to check the specific airport's website for facility and service information.
